question.text: Under Rule 8 of the PML Rules, CTRs and CBWTRs must be furnished to FIU-IND on what frequency?

answer.text: Weekly, by the 7th day of the succeeding week
Monthly, by the 15th day of the succeeding month
Quarterly, by the 15th day of the month succeeding the quarter
Annually, by March 31 of each year

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under Rule 8(1), the Principal Officer shall furnish information regarding CTRs (cash transactions), CBWTRs (cross-border wire transfers), NTRs (non-profit organization transactions), and counterfeit currency transactions every month by the 15th day of the succeeding month.
**The Trap:** The quarterly option (15th of month succeeding the quarter) applies specifically to Immovable Property Reports (IPRs) — not CTRs or CBWTRs. STRs are due within 7 working days. Do not mix up these timelines.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Reporting timelines are a high-frequency test area. Summary: CTR/CBWTR/NTR = monthly by 15th, STR = 7 working days, IPR = quarterly by 15th.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Reports must be furnished online in a standard format prescribed by FIU-IND. The reporting entity must register with FIU-IND for this purpose. Late filing attracts penalties — each day of delay is a separate violation.
